title: Please Choose the Chocolate That Suits Me!
description: A parallel universe story where Adachi never asked Shimamura to exchange chocolates on Valentine's Day. With Nagafuji's help and a boomerang detour, destiny still finds a way.
content:
Narrator (Yashiro):
Allow me to share a small cosmic secret.
Because of certain… circumstances, this universe split into countless parallel worlds. In each of them, life takes a slightly different path.
What you're about to see is one such branch—a world where, on Valentine's Day, Adachi never asked Shimamura to exchange chocolates.

[At the Mall — Adachi's POV]
It was Sunday that year, and the mall was filled with couples waiting for their families, arms linked and smiling beneath the special Valentine's decorations. And there I was, standing alone under a tree set up just for the event.
If only I'd invited Shimamura. That single regret dragged me all the way here.
My plan had been perfect: Invite Shimamura to the mall after school. Look at the posters for the Valentine's event together. Then… maybe… ask her.
But things went wrong the moment I opened my mouth.

'V-Valentine's… h-hic… it says Valentine's… h-hic… Day…'

'Are you okay? Stop talking and breathe.'

'I'm—hic—fi… hic… ne…'

'Wait here. I'll get you water.'

…And that was that.
My heart cracked a little that day, and in the end, I couldn't say anything. Which is why I'm here now, alone.

[Adachi Meets Nagafuji]
Adachi: 'Valentine's Day… trading chocolate with Shimamura…'
Nagafuji: 'Oh? Isn't that Adachi?'
Adachi: 'Na-Nagafuji…'
She looked at the chocolate shelves, then at me. 'Ohh, I get it now.'
Adachi: 'Huh?'
Nagafuji: 'You're here to buy Valentine's chocolate.'
Adachi: 'No! I mean—no… not exactly… I…'
Nagafuji: 'It's for Shimamura, right?'
Adachi: (!!!)
Adachi: 'No—well—not… entirely… you're here to buy chocolate too, Nagafuji?'
Nagafuji: 'Mm-hmm. For Hino. We've been exchanging chocolates for almost ten years now.'
Adachi: '…Ten years.'
Adachi: (Shimamura and I have known each other for less than a year. If we spent ten years together, would that make giving chocolate more natural? I prayed that someday it would—but I couldn't imagine it yet.)
Nagafuji: 'So, Adachi, are you not buying anything?'
Adachi: (…It might not mean anything now. Maybe it won't reach her. But… if it's for our next ten years… then—)
I took a breath.
Adachi: '…I'll buy it. The chocolate.'

[Elsewhere]
Splash.
Hino: 'Got one! Yay! A big catch!'
Yashiro: 'Ooooh, as expected of Hino, the professional fisherwoman!'
Shimamura: 'What kind of title is that…'
Yashiro: 'By the way, Hino—what is that sweet smell coming from your bag?'
Hino: 'Ah, chocolate.'
Yashiro: 'Oh! Is it a surprise gift for me?!'
Shimamura: 'Come to think of it, today is Valentine's Day.'
Yashiro: 'Yes! It's the day one receives chocolate! So, Shimamura, please give me chocolate.'
Shimamura: 'I don't have any right now.'
Yashiro: 'Give me chocolate!'
Shimamura: 'Okay, okay—when we get home. Hino, are you giving Nagafuji chocolate?'
Hino: 'More or less. We've been exchanging since elementary school. Sometimes we compete to see whose chocolate is rarer.'
Shimamura: 'Rarer?'
Hino: 'Super spicy. Or super gross.'
Shimamura: '…The latter is just bullying.'
Hino: 'When her chest first started growing, I gave her boob-shaped chocolate. She seriously smacked me on the head.'
Shimamura: 'That's practically harassment.'
Hino: 'Hehehe. Anyway, Shima—are you giving Adachi chocolate?'
Shimamura: 'Eh? Adachi…?'
(Now that she mentions it… Adachi has been acting weird lately. Probably since early February—when she invited me to the mall and dragged me in front of the Valentine's poster…)
Maybe that's what she wanted to say back then.
Shimamura: '…Maybe I should give her some.'

[Back to Adachi & Nagafuji]
Adachi: 'I bought it… The chocolate.'
Nagafuji: 'Hmm…'
Adachi: 'What?'
Nagafuji: 'I've always wondered—does Valentine's Day have to be about chocolate? Why not… say… a boomerang?'
Adachi: 'A… boomerang?'
Nagafuji: 'Let's make boomerang-gifting a worldwide trend!'
Adachi: 'Uh…'
Nagafuji: 'Oh right, Shimamura gifted her little sister a boomerang for Christmas.'
Adachi: '…Her sister…'
(That's me. Last Christmas, I'd exchanged gifts with Shimamura, but she asked Nagafuji for advice under the pretense of buying something for her 'sister'… And I got… a boomerang.)
Why is Nagafuji obsessed with boomerangs?
Nagafuji: 'Do you still have some money left?'
Adachi: 'A bit…'
Nagafuji: 'Then give Shimamura a boomerang with the chocolate.'
Adachi: 'Why?'
Nagafuji: 'Because then her sister can play too. Fun for everyone!'
Adachi: (Play… with Shimamura… Right. If we had a shared hobby, inviting her out would be easier.)

[Adachi's imagination — A Boomerang Date]
Shimamura: 'How's that, Adachi?'
Adachi: 'Not bad, Shimamura. Watch this.'
Shimamura: '…Adachi, the way you throw it bothers me. Move your feet like this… open your shoulders…'
Adachi: 'W-wah—'
Shimamura: 'Bend your arm ninety degrees—like this.'
Adachi: 'H-hhhaaaa—?!'
Shimamura: 'What was that sound, Adachi?'
Adachi: 'B-b-because—!'
Adachi: (Her face is too close. Her warmth… If I let my guard down, she'll see all the expressions I don't want her to see—)
Shimamura: 'Go on. Try throwing it.'
Adachi: 'O-okay… Hyaaa—!'
Shimamura: 'Oh! It's flying!'
Adachi: 'Ah—'
SPLASH
Shimamura: 'Ah. It fell into the pond.'
Adachi: 'The boomerang… the one Shimamura gave me…'
Yashiro: 'I am the goddess of the pond!'
Shimamura: 'Something came out?!'
Adachi: 'The goddess of the pond…?'
Hino: 'Likewise, I am the pond goddess Hino.'
Nagafuji: 'Likewise, the pond goddess Nagafuji.'
Shimamura: 'That's… a lot of pond goddesses.'
Hino: 'Adachi, did you drop this golden boomerang?'
Nagafuji: 'Or this silver one?'
Adachi: 'I want… the boomerang Shimamura gave me.'
Shimamura: 'No, no, you can take the gold and silver ones too—'
Adachi: 'I just want Shimamura's…'
Shimamura: 'Adachi…'
Yashiro: 'You are honest. Then you may have—'
Adachi: 'I don't want them! Give me back my boomerang!'
Hino: 'Limited-time offer includes diamond boomerang.'
Adachi: 'I… don't… want it! Give me Shimamura's boomerang!'

[Back to Reality]
Adachi (whisper): 'Boomerang… boomerang…'
Nagafuji: 'Adachi?'
Adachi: 'Shimamura's boomerang is the best…'
Nagafuji: 'Adachi. Hello?'
Adachi: 'Ah—!'
Nagafuji: '…You're muttering, spacing out, laughing, sighing… are you okay?'
Adachi: 'I—I'm fine!'
Adachi: (I can daydream about anything, yet somehow it always turns into this. Whenever I'm with Shimamura, I become a total fool.)

[Later — With Shimamura]
Yashiro: 'Mmmf… delicious… The chocolate you gave me tastes amazing!'
Shimamura: 'Glad you like it. Buying two sets was a good idea after all.'
Hino: 'The other one's for Adachi, right?'
Shimamura: 'Yeah. I'll give it to her at school tomorrow.'
Yashiro: 'It smelled tastier than mine…'
Shimamura: 'It is more expensive.'
Yashiro: 'Unfair! Give me that one!'
Shimamura: 'No. This one is for Adachi.'
Adachi: '…Shimamura?'
Shimamura: 'Adachi? Oh—hey.'
Hino: 'Okay, we'll leave you two alone~'
Nagafuji: 'See you tomorrow.'

[Nagafuji & Hino walking home]
Hino: 'Nagafuji, you were with Adacchi earlier?'
Nagafuji: 'We ran into each other at the chocolate display. Here—your Valentine chocolate.'
Hino: 'Ooh, thanks! And here's yours.'
Nagafuji: 'Thanks~ Is yours just normal chocolate this year?'
Hino: 'Yep. Since you like curry and hamburg steak, the regular flavor should be fine, right?'
Nagafuji: 'You always know me best.'
Hino: 'How long do you think we've known each other? And you—did you buy the flavor I like again?'
Nagafuji: 'Maybe~ So, wanna come over?'
Hino: 'Sure. Let's taste-test the chocolates together.'
Nagafuji: 'Let's go, let's go!'

[Back to Adachi & Shimamura]
Adachi: 'The thing you were going to give me… is that… chocolate?'
Shimamura: 'Ah… yeah. Here. Valentine's chocolate.'
Adachi: 'W-wha…! I—I also… this is for you.'
Shimamura: 'For me?'
Adachi: 'Y-yes.'
Shimamura: 'Chocolate?'
Adachi: 'Chocolate.'
Shimamura: 'Valentine's chocolate?'
Adachi: 'N-no—! Not like that—!'
Shimamura: 'What do you mean 'not like that'? Isn't it exactly that?'
Adachi: 'Nyaa—!'
Shimamura: 'Are you a cat?'
Adachi: '…It is. It's for Valentine's Day.'
Shimamura (smiling): 'I see. There's a park nearby.'
Adachi: 'There is…'
Shimamura: 'Want to go eat chocolate together, Adachi?'
Adachi: 'W-wow—yes. Absolutely.'
Shimamura: 'Why so formal? Anyway—oh? Yashiro's gone. Well whatever. Come on, Adachi.'
Adachi: 'Um… is it okay?'
Shimamura: 'It's okay. Oh—holding hands?'
Shimamura: 'It's fine. Here.'
Adachi: 'Ah—!' (very happy)
Shimamura: (We didn't plan anything, yet we still ended up meeting with chocolates in hand. What a strange, wonderful coincidence.)
Adachi: (I'd already given up on spending Valentine's with her. I don't believe in gods… But if there's one that led us to this coincidence, I'd thank them until the day the miracle ends.)
Shimamura: 'Your hand is really warm, Adachi.'
Adachi: 'Shimamura's hands are warm too…'

Narrator (Yashiro):
Now do you see? No matter how the timelines diverge, some things remain constant. Just like Adachi and Shimamura today— that unchanging bond…
That is what we call destiny.
